description_long: This is a rectangular, free-standing street cabinet with a faded orange exterior and a flat top surface. The cabinet stands on two sturdy, curved metal legs, elevating it above the ground. The front face features a door with a handle, a lock, and a white information label marked 'Bitto Elektrotechnik', as well as some graffiti. The side panels are plain except for additional labels, and the rear is unadorned, showing only the orange paint with some scuff marks. The cabinet appears to be made primarily of metal, likely steel, with a powder-coated finish for weather resistance. Its utilitarian design suggests it is used for housing electrical or communication equipment in urban environments.
description: A weathered orange metal street cabinet on metal legs, featuring a front door with a handle, lock, and informational label.
description_view_0: Side view showing a plain orange panel with a white label and a handle, supported by two metal legs.
description_view_1: Front view displaying the main door with a handle, lock, large white label, and graffiti, as well as the curved metal base.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with a similar plain orange panel, white label, and handle, with visible wear at the bottom.
description_view_3: Rear view showing a featureless orange panel with some scuff marks and the metal base visible.
